message:
feat(obligation): Add force_abort_repair for CRDT conflict recovery

Introduces CrdtObligationLedger::force_abort_repair() which collapses
conflicting or linearity-violating obligations into a clean aborted
state by resetting all counters and witnesses to a single local abort.

This differs from record_abort() which appends another abort vote:
- force_abort_repair: nuclear option, clears all state, single witness
- record_abort: incremental, adds to existing counters

RecoveryGovernor now uses force_abort_repair instead of record_abort for
both conflict resolution (auto_resolve_conflicts) and linearity violation
handling (auto_abort_violations). This ensures recovery produces a clean
terminal state rather than accumulating redundant abort votes that could
confuse CRDT merge semantics.

Also fixes import path for RejectReason in distributed/recovery.rs
(moved from decoding module to crate root re-export).

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
